completion_promises.criterion_text: A search returns a single assembled context block (not a raw list).
completion_promises.evidence: assemble_context() returns AssemblyResult dataclass with context_block field containing assembled string

completion_promises.criterion_text: The assembled block never exceeds the token budget, measured with tiktoken cl100k_base.
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 133-144 measure each candidate block with tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base') and only accept when candidate_tokens <= token_budget

completion_promises.criterion_text: A block whose assembled size is exactly the budget is returned in full.
completion_promises.evidence: Line 136 uses <= comparison allowing exactly-at-budget blocks to be accepted; test_exactly_at_budget_returns_full verifies

completion_promises.criterion_text: Content that would push the block past the budget is dropped; lowest-ranked memories omitted.
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 141-144 break iteration when candidate would exceed budget, implementing drop-from-tail behavior on pre-sorted ranked list

completion_promises.criterion_text: A zero token budget returns an empty context block (not an error); coverage reports nothing filled.
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 96-102 explicitly handle zero budget case returning empty AssemblyResult with coverage_score=0.0

completion_promises.criterion_text: A single memory larger than the entire budget is omitted whole, not truncated mid-content.
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 136-144 check entire candidate_block before accepting; never truncates content mid-stream; test_single_memory_larger_than_budget_omitted verifies

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the budget forces a cut, the higher-ranked memory is kept and the lower-ranked one omitted.
completion_promises.evidence: Line 120 iterates ranked_results in order (pre-sorted descending by relevance); line 141 breaks on first overflow, keeping all higher-ranked items

completion_promises.criterion_text: The result reports a coverage score: the fraction (0.0–1.0) of the budget filled, plus the set of payload types that contributed to the block.
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 150-161 calculate coverage_score = tokens_used / token_budget and extract contributing_types set from natural_keys; AssemblyResult exposes both fields

completion_promises.criterion_text: A search that cannot fill the budget reports partial coverage honestly (not padded).
completion_promises.evidence: Lines 150-153 calculate coverage from actual tokens_used without padding; test_partial_coverage_reported_honestly verifies coverage < 1.0 when budget not filled

completion_promises.criterion_text: Repeated searches over an unchanged corpus return an identical assembled block (assembly is deterministic).
completion_promises.evidence: No randomness in implementation; consistent iteration order over ranked_results; tiktoken encoding is deterministic; test_deterministic_assembly verifies repeated calls return identical results

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/fleet_memory/retrieval/ returned 'All checks passed!'
